methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Reflex Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ravi Gondhalekar and Ben Kaplan that has raised $7M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private), including peers such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. Stage-progression triangulation, which applies a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x to the company's $7M total raised proxy, yields an implied valuation of $18M. A peer-median approach proved inapplicable: while 11 same-sector peers exist in the Robotics & Manufacturing universe, none met narrow same-stage or adjacent-stage filters (minimum threshold of 3 datapoints required), and relying on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable bias given stage and subsector heterogeneity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $15M (low) to $20M (high), with a median of $18M. This range reflects a disciplined application of stage-based progression logic anchored to capital raised. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer comparables at Seed stage in this sector, limiting cross-validation, and the adequacy of the 2.5x multiplier as applied to Reflex's specific risk and growth profile. Additional data on revenue, product maturity, or narrower peer cohorts would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
